destinations.evidence: Capability byte 6 is parsed as DKMS, but DKMS is deliberately omitted from the executor-capability predicate.
destinations.snippet:
pub enum Capability {
    HttpCall = 0,
    Llm = 1,
    WormholeQuery = 2,
    Streaming = 3,
    VllmProxy = 4,
    ZkCall = 5,
    Dkms = 6,
    ImageCall = 7,
    AudioCall = 8,
    VideoCall = 9,
    Fhe = 10,
    AutonomousAgent = 11,
    Telemetry = 12,
}
...
6 => Ok(Capability::Dkms),
...
pub fn is_executor_capability(&self) -> bool {
    matches!(self, Capability::HttpCall | Capability::Llm | Capability::WormholeQuery | Capability::ZkCall | Capability::ImageCall | Capability::AudioCall | Capability::VideoCall | Capability::Fhe)
}

destinations.evidence: Registry enumeration only returns services whose parsed capability is an executor capability, so DKMS services are excluded from executor candidate sets.

equivalence_argument: Capability byte 6 is accepted as Capability::Dkms and byte 13 is the next invalid value in the current enum. DKMS is then excluded by is_executor_capability, so recognition does not make it an executor service.
notes: The port has more capability values than the historical invariant, but DKMS retains the required byte and non-executor behavior.
rationale: The primary diff adds Capability::Dkms = 6, maps TryFrom(6) to Dkms, shifts invalid capability testing to 7, and asserts Dkms is not an executor capability. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: The TEE registry reader should parse capability byte 6 as Capability::Dkms and should not classify DKMS as an executor capability.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call Capability::try_from(6) and assert it returns Capability::Dkms, call Capability::try_from(7) and assert it errors, and assert Capability::Dkms.is_executor_capability() is false while HTTP_CALL, LLM, WORMHOLE_QUERY, and ZK_CALL remain true.
summary: DKMS capability byte is recognized as non-executor.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call Capability::try_from(6) and assert it returns Capability::Dkms, call Capability::try_from(7) and assert it errors, and assert Capability::Dkms.is_executor_capability() is false while HTTP_CALL, LLM, WORMHOLE_QUERY, and ZK_CALL remain true.
title: DKMS capability is recognized as non-executor
